data.body:
REMEDIATION of plan-review findings on the 10 WS stories (review run 2026-07; NOT re-reviewed per instruction).

Review outcome first: DET floor (P1-P8) PASSED on all 10. WS1+WS4 verdict=PASS; the other 8 came back INDETERMINATE due to HTTP 429 rate-limiting on the agentic verifier (coverage.verify_failed=true) -- Pass-1 findings preserved unverified, NOT confirmed defects. So remediation treated all LLM findings as advisory.

Universal fixes applied to all 10:
- P9 (no file_impact) x10: set-file-impact recorded on every story with verified real paths.
- E6/F1/F4 hedged ACs: every 'eval confirms'/'drift-parity stays green' AC rewritten to name a concrete proving command (pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k <case>; python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing; grep/jq/diff structural checks).
- Provenance cluster (E4/G1G2/T8 'identifier not in codebase'): added a ## Provenance note to each story stating G-*/FP-*/R-*/WS-* are gap-report/session tags (this log + ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md), not code symbols; concrete code targets named inline + in file_impact.

Substantive per-ticket corrections from code-grounded findings:
- WS1: sub-answers live in shared review_kernel (GRADED_BINARY verify.py + validity decide.py), not plan_review-local; respects_artifact_altitude clarified as a graded validity input (not a veto); 'both variants' = the 2 verifier prompt files.
- WS2: exec:DET SCANS CODE (Engine B), not plan-prose, AND DET findings BYPASS Pass 2 -> hedge-detector mechanism reframed as an open design decision (exec:DET-into-verifier vs 1-TURN finder); committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim marked as introduced by WS1 + depends_on WS1 link added.
- WS4: DET_CHECKS frozen P1-P9 (ADR 0016, test-asserted) -> extend p6, do not add P10.
- WS5: T9 RETIRE rejection moved out of AC list (orphan-AC fix); eval-test consumers enumerated; nine E6 patterns + four T3 classes required to be enumerated in-prompt.
- WS6: exact one-way blast_radius edit locations named (both verifier prompts + SeverityAttrs doc); two-directional eval AC.
- WS7: scope = the 41 plan_review_*.md (reviewer's ~59 counted code-review too); NO shared-scaffolding layer exists -> must introduce a shared-preamble composition point first; forward-looking rule is in F1 only (not E1).
- WS8: MOVE_REGISTRY canonical in review_kernel/coach.py AND mirrored in docs/plan-review-gate.md table -> lockstep update; F1-blocking phrasing clarified.
- WS9: findings emitted by pass1_chunk AND pass1_container AND pass1_isf (all three must stamp cohort); _slim is a key-allowlist; out-of-tree callers eval_solver.py + criterion_preview.py flagged.
- WS10: R-3 vs R-5 flagged as splittable distinct deliverables; parity gate only covers routing JSON today -> must be EXTENDED for the guide; explain error-states specified.

Not re-reviewed (per instruction). Rate-limit-induced INDETERMINATEs were an artifact of running the batch too hot, not ticket quality.
